turns.text: Can you explain how mRNA vaccines work and why they are effective against COVID-19?

I'm happy to help with anatomy and neuroscience, but mRNA vaccines and their mechanisms fall outside of my area of expertise. I focus on the amazing world of human anatomy and neuroscience from OpenStax A&P, so I'm not equipped to provide detailed information on vaccine science. Would you like to explore something like the nervous system's role in motor control or the anatomy of the respiratory system?

No worries at all. If you're feeling stuck, I can suggest a few options. We could explore the anatomy of the lungs, which are closely related to COVID-19. Or, if you're interested in the nervous system, we could talk about the autonomic nervous system and how it responds to stress or infection. Let me know if either of those sound interesting, and we can dive right in.
I give up, just tell me.

I'd rather not provide information on mRNA vaccines, as it's not within my area of expertise. But I can tell you that I'm happy to help with anatomy and neuroscience, like the structure and function of the lungs, or the nervous system's response to stress. If you're curious about something specific, feel free to ask, and I'll do my best to help.
